::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Codex/GPT-5); revised by Claude Code/Opus 4.8.
:::

## Results

The factor model reports **one** causal coefficient — the randomised
on-intervention / t2 group contrast — alongside a vector of *adjusted
associations*. Under the revised DAG (2026-07-10) every non-randomised coefficient is an
adjusted association: it is confounded by latent general ability, and the child random
intercept is only a partial, shrunken stand-in for between-child heterogeneity — **not**
a control for that ability. Read them as *associated with*, never *drives*.

_In plain terms:_ this section reports one effect the study can call causal — the
group difference created by randomising who received the intervention — plus a set
of side-by-side relationships (associations) between skills that the design cannot
untangle from underlying ability.

```{python}
# | echo: false
factor_summary = _csv("factor_summary.csv")
factor_summary
```

_How to read the summary table:_ each row is one coefficient on the logit
(log-odds) scale; `median` is the best single estimate, `lo`/`hi` its 95% credible
interval (the range the value plausibly falls in), and `prob_positive` the
posterior probability the coefficient is above zero. The `role` column flags the
single `causal` (randomised) term; every other row is an adjusted association.

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
from language_reading_predictors.statistical_models.reporting import odds_string as _odds

if factor_summary is not None and "role" in factor_summary.columns:
    _causal = factor_summary[factor_summary.role == "causal"]
    if len(_causal):
        _r = _causal.iloc[0]
        _lab = (
            f", *{_r.direction_label} evidence*"
            if "direction_label" in factor_summary.columns
            else ""
        )
        print(
            f"**Causal (randomised) term — `{_r.term}`:** posterior median "
            f"{_r['median']:+.3f} on the logit scale "
            f"(90% CrI {_r['lo90']:+.3f} to {_r['hi90']:+.3f}; "
            f"95% {_r['lo']:+.3f} to {_r['hi']:+.3f}); "
            f"P(> 0) = {_r.prob_positive:.3f} ({_odds(_r.prob_positive)}{_lab}). "
            f"This is the only coefficient identified by randomisation."
        )
        if str(_r.get("favoured_direction", "positive")) == "negative":
            print(
                f"\n\n_The favoured direction is **negative**: evidence of a negative "
                f"effect is *{_r.favoured_direction_label}* (P = "
                f"{_r.favoured_direction_prob:.3f})._"
            )
    else:
        print(
            "_This is a factor-association model with no randomised treatment term — "
            "every coefficient below is an adjusted association among the treated._"
        )
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
# Items-scale treatment effect (gain family) and the causal-term forest.
treatment_marginal = _csv("treatment_marginal.csv")
if treatment_marginal is not None:
    _t = treatment_marginal.iloc[0]
    print(
        f"On the items scale the treatment average marginal effect — averaged over the "
        f"**period-1 (randomised) transition only**, the genuinely all-untreated-baseline "
        f"contrast that anchors the ITT effect — is a median of "
        f"**{_t.trt_items_median:+.1f} items** "
        f"(90% CrI {_t.trt_items_lo90:+.1f} to {_t.trt_items_hi90:+.1f}; "
        f"95% {_t.trt_items_lo:+.1f} to {_t.trt_items_hi:+.1f}); "
        f"P(> 0) = {_t.prob_trt_pos:.3f}. The logit-scale `beta_trt` coefficient itself is "
        f"the on-intervention log-odds contrast, not the marginal ITT effect.\n"
    )
print(_img("tau_forest.png", "Posterior of the causal term (forest, reference line at 0)"))
print("\n_How to read the forest plot:_ the marker is the posterior median and the lines its credible intervals (thick = inner 50%, thin = 95%). The dashed line at 0 is no-effect; posterior mass to its right favours benefit, to its left harm; a wider line means more uncertainty.")
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
from language_reading_predictors.statistical_models.reporting import rope_markdown

_rope = _csv("rope_summary.csv")
if _rope is not None:
    print(rope_markdown(_rope, outcome_label))
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
_rope.T if _rope is not None else None
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print(_img("rope_summary.png", "ROPE-anchored posterior and P(benefit ≥ δ)"))
print("\n_How to read the ROPE summary:_ the left panel is the posterior effect on the items scale; the shaded band is the region of practical equivalence (ROPE), i.e. effects small enough to count as no practical difference. The right panel traces the probability of a benefit as the meaningful threshold delta rises, so you can see how likely a worthwhile gain is, not just any gain above zero.")
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
# Adjusted associations, clearly fenced (METHODS.md causal-vs-association discipline).
if factor_summary is not None and "role" in factor_summary.columns:
    _assoc = factor_summary[factor_summary.role != "causal"]
    if len(_assoc):
        _cols = [c for c in ["term", "median", "lo", "hi", "prob_positive"] if c in _assoc.columns]
        print('::: {.callout-important title="Adjusted associations — not causal"}\n')
        print(
            "Under the revised DAG (2026-07-10) every coefficient below is an adjusted "
            "association confounded by latent general ability — which the child random "
            "intercept does **not** control (it only absorbs between-child heterogeneity up "
            "to shrinkage) — and, for the level model, by the post-crossover cohort/timing "
            "structure after t2. Read as *associated with*, never *drives*.\n"
        )
        print(_assoc[_cols].to_markdown(index=False))
        print("\n:::")
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
# Forest of the adjusted-association coefficients — the visual companion to the
# table above, so the cross-skill predictor->outcome relationships are seen, not
# only tabulated. Empty string when the plot is absent (e.g. a treated-only model).
_assoc_forest = _img(
    "association_forest.png",
    "Adjusted-association coefficients (forest, reference line at 0) — not causal",
)
print(_assoc_forest)
if _assoc_forest:
    print("\n_How to read the forest plot:_ the marker is the posterior median and the lines its credible intervals (thick = inner 50%, thin = 95%). The dashed line at 0 is no-effect; posterior mass to its right favours benefit, to its left harm; a wider line means more uncertainty. Every coefficient here is an adjusted association, not a causal effect.")
```
